contracts.title: Normative contracts index
contracts.summary: Developer-facing index of binding slice and runtime contracts.
contracts.discovery_reason: A: explicit governance index path + normative vocabulary in prose

contracts.title: Public HTTP API (OpenAPI 3)
contracts.summary: Bounded machine-readable schema for the Flask backend /api/v1 inventory; subordinate to higher-order runtime/API authority for overlapping run semantics.
contracts.notes: Authority is bounded to the packaged backend /api/v1 schema surface. Higher-order runtime/API authority still lives in the canonical runtime contract family.
contracts.discovery_reason: A: canonical OpenAPI document under docs/api/ (bounded backend API schema, not universal runtime authority)

contracts.title: ADR-0001: Runtime authority in world-engine
contracts.summary: Accepted runtime authority decision: world-engine owns authoritative live narrative execution.
contracts.notes: Authority record outranks slice-level contracts if host ownership claims conflict.

contracts.title: ADR-0002: Backend session / transitional runtime quarantine
contracts.summary: Accepted quarantine/retirement policy for backend-local session and runtime-shaped surfaces.
contracts.notes: Quarantine record governs compatibility and retirement, not live session authority.

contracts.title: ADR-0003: Single canonical scene identity surface
contracts.summary: Accepted slice ADR choosing one owned GoC scene-identity surface across compile, AI guidance, and commit.
contracts.notes: Slice-specific ADR below runtime authority tier.

contracts.title: Runtime authority and state flow
contracts.summary: Consolidated runtime host/state progression contract for live play and transitional backend surfaces.

contracts.title: Backend runtime classification
contracts.summary: Classification contract separating backend-local volatile surfaces from authoritative runtime execution.

contracts.title: Canonical Runtime Contract (Nested Run V1)
contracts.summary: Binding producer/consumer payload contract for play-service run create/detail/terminate envelopes.
contracts.scope: world-engine HTTP to backend consumer seam

contracts.title: Player Input Interpretation Contract
contracts.summary: Structured interpretation contract for raw player text, explicit commands, ambiguity, and delivery hints.
contracts.scope: authoritative and preview interpretation of player input

contracts.title: GoC Vertical Slice Contract
contracts.summary: Primary MVP vertical-slice contract defining GoC scope, YAML authority, and runtime bridge expectations.

contracts.title: GoC Canonical Turn Contract
contracts.summary: Binding GoC turn envelope schema for validation, commit, diagnostics, and review.

contracts.title: GoC Gate Scoring Policy
contracts.summary: Gate/scoring policy for GoC evaluation, fallback classification, and experience acceptance evidence.
contracts.scope: GoC gate scoring and evidence policy

contracts.title: Writers’ Room and publishing flow
contracts.summary: Backend-first Writers’ Room review/publishing workflow with a bounded retrieval overlap seam; AI outputs remain recommendation-only until human/backend publishing governance applies changes.
contracts.notes: Intentional overlap with RAG is limited to retrieval_context_provider and context_pack_assembly support; publish_authority, canonical promotion, and runtime truth remain separate.

contracts.title: RAG governance
contracts.summary: Retrieval governance contract separating retrieved context from authored canon and committed runtime state, including a bounded Writers’ Room overlap seam at retrieval/context-pack support only.
contracts.scope: retrieval domains, governance lanes, and context assembly
contracts.notes: Writers’ Room support is limited to retrieval_context_provider and context_pack_assembly; retrieval output remains recommendation/context support and not publish_authority or runtime truth.

contracts.title: Test orchestration and suite runner
contracts.summary: Repository-level testing governance anchor describing orchestrated suite execution and environment preflight expectations.
contracts.notes: Verification governance anchor, not a product/runtime authority contract.

contracts.title: StoryRuntimeManager implementation surface
contracts.summary: Observed authoritative runtime manager for story sessions, turn execution, retrieval wiring, and diagnostics.

contracts.title: World-engine HTTP API surface
contracts.summary: Observed FastAPI play-service surface for runs and story sessions.

contracts.title: Backend session routes surface
contracts.summary: Observed backend session API surface explicitly labeled non-authoritative and partly world-engine-bridged.

contracts.title: Backend session store surface
contracts.summary: Observed volatile in-memory runtime session registry retained for tests, MCP, and transitional operator flows.

contracts.title: Backend session service surface
contracts.summary: Observed session service bridge for backend-local SessionState bootstrap and deferred W3.2 behavior.

contracts.title: Backend world-engine console routes
contracts.summary: Observed admin/JWT proxy surface for controlled play-service observation and operations.

contracts.title: Backend Writers’ Room routes
contracts.summary: Observed backend review/decision route surface for Writers’ Room workflow execution.

contracts.title: Shared input interpreter surface
contracts.summary: Observed implementation of structured natural-language and command interpretation.

contracts.title: GoC scene identity implementation surface
contracts.summary: Observed single owned scene identity mapping for GoC guidance and escalation vocabulary.

contracts.title: GoC YAML authority surface
contracts.summary: Observed YAML authority loader/re-export surface consuming the canonical scene identity module.

contracts.title: RAG implementation surface
contracts.summary: Observed retrieval corpus, request, ranking, and governance implementation for runtime and Writers’ Room callers.

contracts.title: Backend game service seam
contracts.summary: Observed backend consumer/proxy seam for world-engine run and story-session APIs.

contracts.title: Repository pytest runner implementation
contracts.summary: Observed multi-suite pytest orchestrator used as the root verification launcher.

contracts.title: GoC scene identity contract tests
contracts.summary: Verification suite asserting canonical scene-id mapping and duplicate-definition protection for ADR-0003.

contracts.title: Input interpreter contract tests
contracts.summary: Verification suite covering structured kind selection and runtime delivery hints for player input interpretation.

contracts.title: Experience score matrix CLI tests
contracts.summary: Verification suite for the G9 threshold validator and canonical six-scenario GoC experience matrix ordering/rules.

contracts.title: Repository documented paths smoke test
contracts.summary: Verification guard asserting high-visibility documented module/test paths resolve on disk.

contracts.title: World-engine story runtime API tests
contracts.summary: Verification suite for story-session lifecycle, retrieval fields, and interpreted-input behavior.

contracts.title: Operational governance runtime (runbook)
contracts.summary: Operator-facing procedures and guarantees for runtime governance controls.
contracts.discovery_reason: B: explicit operations markdown under docs/operations/

contracts.title: JSON schema: ai_story_output.schema.json
contracts.summary: Shared machine-readable schema constraining data exchanged across components.
contracts.discovery_reason: B: top-level schemas/*.json with bounded scan (max 2 files per pass ceiling)

contracts.title: JSON schema: content_module.schema.json
contracts.summary: Shared machine-readable schema constraining data exchanged across components.
contracts.discovery_reason: B: top-level schemas/*.json with bounded scan (max 2 files per pass ceiling)

contracts.title: Postmanify sync task (OpenAPI → Postman)
contracts.summary: Procedure for regenerating Postman collections and manifest from the canonical OpenAPI anchor.
contracts.discovery_reason: A: postmanify task markdown under fy-suite (explicit OpenAPI linkage expected in prose)

contracts.title: Docify documentation check task
contracts.summary: Procedure and inputs for repository documentation quality audits (Python/docstring drift).
contracts.discovery_reason: A: docify governance task markdown under fy-suite

contracts.title: Despaghettify spaghetti setup (normative)
contracts.summary: Numeric triggers and governance rules for structural execution hub.
contracts.discovery_reason: A: explicit canonical / normative sections in spaghetti-setup.md

contracts.title: Contractify suite charter
contracts.summary: Defines suite scope, truth model, drift classes, and integration with sibling fy hubs.

projections.title: Developer onboarding runtime authority summary
projections.evidence: Onboarding links runtime authority and GoC contract family as navigation surface.

projections.title: Player-facing GoC guide
projections.evidence: Player guide summarizes the slice and turn contract family for a non-authority audience.

projections.title: Admin publishing guide
projections.evidence: Admin guide summarizes Writers’ Room / publishing flow for operational consumers.

projections.title: API README projection
projections.evidence: API README is governed as a projection-layer overview rather than a higher-order contract.

projections.title: API reference projection
projections.evidence: Human-readable backend reference remains a convenience projection of the OpenAPI/schema layer and validated route code.

projections.title: API Postman guide projection
projections.evidence: Postman guide is a lower-order operational projection and not a primary schema anchor.

projections.title: OpenAPI and API explorer strategy projection
projections.evidence: Strategy doc records current projection limits and future re-audit checks without becoming the canonical API contract.

projections.title: Postman README projection
projections.evidence: Postman README explains generated collections as operational projections of the packaged OpenAPI artifact.

projections.title: Audience doc: README.md
projections.evidence: Path under docs/easy or docs/start-here; treated as projection hub by policy

The technical runtime authority page expands ADR-0001 into an operational ownership matrix and lifecycle narrative.
The backend classification page clarifies what ADR-0001 forbids inside Flask as live runtime authority.
The classification document turns ADR-0002 quarantine language into concrete package/module labels.
The nested-run producer/consumer contract assumes a single authoritative run host chosen by ADR-0001.
The backend consumer rules in Nested Run V1 sit beside ADR-0002’s prohibition on treating backend-local session surfaces as equivalent runtime truth.
ADR-0002 explicitly states that ADR-0001 remains the sole normative owner of runtime authority and narrows only backend session-surface quarantine and labeling under that model.
ADR-0003 explicitly states that it does not define runtime authority and instead applies scene-identity continuity rules under ADR-0001.
Gate scoring references turn-envelope semantics and preview/productive classification from the canonical turn contract.
Gate scoring depends on the slice’s scope, vocabulary, and failure-mode boundaries.
The Writers’ Room workflow overlaps with RAG only at retrieval_context_provider and context_pack_assembly, while publish_authority, canonical promotion, and runtime committed truth remain separate.
RAG defines a Writers’ Room retrieval lane and context-pack support role, but remains subordinate to backend/admin publishing governance and world-engine runtime truth.
StoryRuntimeManager is named as the first code anchor on the runtime-authority page and wires runtime sessions, retrieval, and turn execution.
Session routes embody the backend-local quarantine by returning warnings and bridging to the world-engine for authoritative turns.
Admin console routes are the documented compat/operator surface in ADR-0002 Appendix A.
Writers’ Room review/decision routes expose the backend-first workflow described in the publishing-flow document.
ai_stack/rag.py is the primary implementation anchor listed in RAG.md.
Onboarding doc condenses the runtime authority contract for navigation.
Player guide summarizes GoC slice rules and canonical turn semantics for a lower-authority audience.
Admin publishing guide restates the backend-first publishing path.
AI system overview reuses RAG governance as a summarized system map.
The packaged backend OpenAPI artifact is bounded to route/schema inventory and remains subordinate to the higher-order canonical runtime contract for overlapping run lifecycle semantics.
API README is explicitly governed as a projection-layer overview of the packaged OpenAPI artifact.
API reference is explicitly governed as a convenience projection of the packaged OpenAPI artifact.
Where the reference mentions run lifecycle payload semantics, it remains subordinate to the canonical runtime contract.
Postman guide is governed as an operational projection rooted in the packaged OpenAPI artifact.
Strategy doc records the projection layer around the packaged OpenAPI artifact without becoming the primary contract itself.
Postman README classifies generated collections as convenience projections derived from the packaged OpenAPI artifact.
Manifest openapi_path binds generation input
Postmanify emits collection from same OpenAPI revision fingerprint

OpenAPI describes Flask `/api/v1` routes implemented under backend/.

precedence_rules.summary: Highest-order runtime authority and boundary contracts. These outrank slice detail, implementation observations, and projections when authority clashes are reviewed.
Binding MVP / slice contracts and accepted slice-scoped ADRs. These govern GoC behavior beneath the runtime authority layer.
Observed code surfaces that embody or operationalize contracts but do not replace normative authority.
Test and verification surfaces that support claims about implementation and documented paths.
Lower-weight audience projections and convenience summaries. Useful for navigation, never equal to runtime authority or slice contracts.

manual_unresolved_areas.summary: Backend transitional retirement remains intentionally unresolved for the backend-local trio, but the boundary is now closure-prepared: session_routes, session_store, and session_service remain retirement-open while world_engine_console_routes stays separately retained operator support.
manual_unresolved_areas.notes: Governed as quarantine/compat today. Honest closure still requires caller/test/operator dependency evidence, explicit reclassification or removal, and proof that retained operator support is not being mistaken for the retirement-open trio.

manual_unresolved_areas.summary: API-facing docs and Postman assets are intentionally governed as lower-order projections of the bounded OpenAPI schema and higher-order canonical runtime/API contracts; partial schema depth and projection drift risk remain visible.
manual_unresolved_areas.notes: Not a contradiction today; keep visible so convenience docs, generated collections, or partial schema coverage do not drift into a second competing API truth layer.

manual_unresolved_areas.summary: Writers’ Room workflow and RAG governance intentionally overlap only at retrieval_context_provider and context_pack_assembly support; publish_authority, canonical promotion, and runtime truth remain separate and must stay explicitly reviewed.
manual_unresolved_areas.notes: Not a contradiction today; keep reviewable so future retrieval write-backs, auto-publish semantics, or runtime write-through paths do not flatten authority boundaries. Re-audit must verify that retrieval output remains support/context only.

disclaimer: Heuristic drift is evidence for review, not automatic ground truth. Normative authority outranks observed implementation in governance decisions.
